content:
# elizaos-plugins/registry Daily Update (Apr 28, 2026)

## OVERVIEW 
Development on April 28, 2026, focused on dependency management and infrastructure maintenance. Efforts centered on resolving a `bun install` failure on the `develop` branch caused by a missing package version, with a stack of PRs prepared to update core dependencies including TypeScript, Node.js, and Rollup.

## KEY TECHNICAL DEVELOPMENTS

**Dependency Updates and Infrastructure**
*   A stack of PRs has been prepared to address pending dependency updates, including TypeScript v6, Node.js v24, Rollup 4.60.2, Rimraf v6, and @capacitor/core 8.3.1.
*   Identified a critical blocker for `bun install` on `develop` due to an unpublished transitive dependency pin in `@elizaos/plugin-todo`.
*   Noted CI workflow issues, specifically that `ci.yaml` has been inactive since February and current quality checks fail to invoke `bun install` or `tsc`.

**Script and Configuration Maintenance**
*   Identified a path error in `bun run generate:types`, which currently targets a non-existent directory (`packages/@schemas` instead of `packages/schemas`).
*   Confirmed that previous Renovate lookup-failure warnings for deleted files are expected to clear automatically.

## NEWLY OPENED PULL REQUESTS
*   None.

## CLOSED ISSUES
*   None.

## NEW ISSUES
*   None.

## ACTIVE ISSUES
*   **[#79](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/registry/issues/79) Dependency Dashboard**
    *   The dashboard is currently tracking a stack of PRs ([#7146](https://github.com/elizaOS/eliza/pull/7146), [#7147](https://github.com/elizaOS/eliza/pull/7147), [#7148](https://github.com/elizaOS/eliza/pull/7148), [#7149](https://github.com/elizaOS/eliza/pull/7149), [#7150](https://github.com/elizaOS/eliza/pull/7150), [#7151](https://github.com/elizaOS/eliza/pull/7151)).
    *   Progress is gated by the resolution of [#7146](https://github.com/elizaOS/eliza/pull/7146) to fix the `bun install` breakage.
    *   TypeScript v6 testing revealed a pre-existing typecheck failure in `@elizaos/autonomous` related to a missing import, which is being tracked as a separate follow-up.
